pr.title: Match a bunch of "duplicate" functions with Claude
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Large decomp-matching PR that used `table-typer dups` plus Claude to match many functions with nearly identical assembly to already-matched duplicates. The work spanned 131 files across fighter, item, stage, game-mode, library, and menu code, replacing many `/// #symbol` stubs with C implementations and adding the headers/prototypes/includes needed for those matches. The PR is explicitly described as a partial batch of remaining duplicate functions, with the author emphasizing that duplicate matches are not safe cut-and-paste because equivalent assembly can involve different local types. Review focused on correcting AI-generated type/float/include mistakes: using item-specific `itemVar` structs instead of borrowing `linkhookshot`, preserving floating-point literal forms for `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, using the right baselib include path, and fixing mistaken generator/app-srt field interpretation.
what_changed: Replaced many unmatched function markers with C implementations derived from already-matched functions that had nearly identical assembly.
Expanded fighter matches heavily, especially in `ftKb_Init.c`, `ftCh_Init.c`, `ftCo_Attack100.c`, and `ftPp_SpecialS.c`.
Matched numerous item functions across Pokémon, common items, character projectiles/items, and related headers.
Matched several stage route/generator-related functions in files such as `grkinokoroute.c`, `grbigblueroute.c`, `grvenom.c`, `grcorneria.c`, `grcastle.c`, and `grgarden.c`.
Added or adjusted function declarations, static prototypes, extern float declarations, and includes required by the newly matched functions.
Refactored `ftCo_ThrownKirby.c` to reuse an existing inline/helper-style routine (`inlineA0(gobj)`) instead of keeping the duplicated velocity/timer logic inline.
Used pragmas such as `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` in at least one matched `ftCo_Attack100.c` function to preserve matching behavior.

After review, fixed at least some AI-introduced mistakes around typed fields and prior duplicate assumptions, including the author noting that previously matched duplicates in `grCastle_801D0A9C` and `grGarden_80203560` had the same issue.
smart_moves: Used `table-typer dups` to find functions with nearly identical assembly where one copy was already matched, turning a broad matching problem into a guided duplicate-transfer workflow.
Did not treat duplicate assembly as blind copy-paste; the PR body explicitly notes that local types can differ even when assembly is nearly identical.
Fed both the already-matched duplicate and `decomp.py` output into Claude, using AI for repetitive manual adaptation while retaining human/reviewer validation.
Batched many small duplicate-derived matches across subsystems, making broad progress on scattered stubs that would otherwise be tedious to match one at a time.
Propagated necessary header/prototype/include changes with the function matches instead of leaving implicit declarations or local-only knowledge.

Accepted reviewer corrections where Claude had overgeneralized types, with the author explicitly acknowledging the weakness and saying the process had instructions that the model sometimes failed to follow.
decomp_lessons: Nearly identical assembly is strong evidence for a duplicate function, but matching still requires checking type context, struct unions, field names, literal types, and include ownership.
AI-assisted duplicate matching can accelerate repetitive decomp work, but reviewers should specifically audit for lazy type reuse from the source duplicate.
When matching `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls, preserve float literal forms like `0.0f` and `1.0f` rather than relying on integer-looking constants; reviewers flagged this repeatedly.
Do not borrow an unrelated `itemVar` sub-struct just because it matches layout; item code should get its own itemVar struct when the item-specific meaning differs.
Generator/app-srt code needs careful struct interpretation; a duplicate may compile/match while semantically naming or casting the wrong field.
Include path conventions matter in this codebase: reviewer feedback specifically preferred angle-bracket include form `<baselib/psstructs.h>` instead of a quoted baselib include.
Previously matched duplicates can carry forward the same mistaken interpretation; when a duplicate-derived fix is found, audit the known source duplicates too.
Small wrapper functions and thin forwarding functions are good duplicate-match candidates, but visibility (`static` vs exported prototype) and header declarations still need to match call sites.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions continue to use address-derived names such as `fn_800DA004`, `fn_8015746C`, and `grKinokoRoute_802086EC` when no semantic name is established.
Fighter action callbacks follow subsystem/action suffix conventions such as `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Crazy Hand functions use `ftCh_` or `ftCh_Init_` prefixes, with motion-state-oriented names like `ftCh_WaitSweep_Anim`, `ftCh_BackPunch_Phys`, and `ftCh_TagCancel_Anim`.
Common fighter functions in `ftCo_Attack100.c` retain `ftCo_` prefixes for shared fighter behavior, including capture/catch-related names.
Stage route functions use stage-specific prefixes such as `grKinokoRoute_`, `grCastle_`, and `grGarden_`.
Reviewer feedback implies item-specific state should be represented by item-specific `itemVar` structs rather than reusing another item's struct name such as `linkhookshot`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `table-typer dups` was used to identify nearly identical assembly among functions where at least one duplicate already matched.
The workflow used the matched duplicate plus `decomp.py` output as inputs to Claude to adapt the implementation to each target function.
Manual validation remained necessary because duplicated assembly can arise with different involved types or struct members.
Wrapper functions were matched by recognizing direct delegation patterns, for example `ftCo_CatchPull_Phys` calling `ftCo_Catch_Phys` and collision functions forwarding into shared helpers.
Inline/helper extraction was used in `ftCo_ThrownKirby.c`, replacing repeated velocity adjustment code with `inlineA0(gobj)` while preserving surrounding behavior.
Stack-padding and inlining controls were still used where necessary, evidenced by `PAD_STACK(8)` and `#pragma dont_inline on` in matched fighter code.
Float-vs-int literal spelling was treated as match-relevant in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` argument lists.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: The matched logic should use its own itemVar struct and not include or reuse `itlinkhookshot`/`linkhookshot` state.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the itemVar issue as a good catch and noted that Claude sometimes gets lazy despite explicit instructions.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Use floating-point arguments in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, e.g. `fp->cur_anim_frame, 1.0f, 0.0f, NULL`.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Declare motion-state arguments as `0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f, NULL` where appropriate.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged a generator interpretation as nonsense and clarified that the code was referencing `appsrt->scale`.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that previously matched duplicates `grCastle_801D0A9C` and `grGarden_80203560` had the same appsrt issue and said all would be fixed.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs by lukechampine that continue the `table-typer dups` plus Claude duplicate-function workflow.
Search for fixes touching `grCastle_801D0A9C`, `grGarden_80203560`, or `grKinokoRoute_802086EC` related to `appsrt->scale`.
Search item files for remaining uses of another item's `itemVar` sub-struct names such as `linkhookshot` after AI-assisted duplicate matches.
Search review comments mentioning `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` and `1.0f`/`0.0f` to collect literal-format matching rules.
Find PRs that add or adjust `<baselib/psstructs.h>` includes for generator/app-srt structure access.
Audit later duplicate-derived matches for repeated Claude mistakes around structs, float literals, and include style.
